Dept.505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceeding recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:56:34 a.m., pausing at 10:13:00 a.m., commencing at 10:25:40 a.m. and concluding at 10:2615 a.m.(D505)

Shirean Kennedy, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff Pinqui Kang.
Plaintiff Pinqui Kang was placed under oath and testified.
Plaintiff's Complaint for breach of contract filed on April 1, 2021.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant served on April 21, 2021.
Default entered as to Defendant Tuanfei Zhang on June 23, 2021.
Order denying Defendant's motion to set aside default filed on September 21, 2022.
Dismissal filed as to Defendant Does 1 to 100 on April 3, 2023.
Application for waiver of in-person witness requirement filed April 24, 2023.
Notice of hearing and Proof of service by mail as to Defendant filed September 26, 2023.

Plaintiff's Counsel to summarize the case and provides offer of proof to the Court.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibit/s for the Court 's review:
Exhibit 1: IOU note.
Exhibit 2: Checks issued by the Plaintiff.

The Court clarifies the translation of the agreement written in the Chinese language.

The Court confirms with Plaintiff that counsel's statements are true and correct.

The Court examines Plaintiff's witness.

Based on the oral contract, testimony and evidence presented, consideration and good cause appearing the court rules in favor of the Plaintiff Pinqui Kang and against Defendant Tuanfei Zhang.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and submit proposed order.

Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Elizabeth Macayan, Esq. (415.433.3900; emacayan@ongaropc.com) of Ongaro PC for Plaintiff.
Richard E. Oriahki, Esq. (415.251.6641) for and with Defendant Tuanfei Zhang.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TUANFEI ZHANG'S Motion To Set Aside Default.

Defendant Tuanfei Zhang's motion to set aside default is denied.

First, Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) does not provide Defendant with a basis for relief. A motion for relief under that statute "shall . . . be made within a reasonable time, in no case exceeding six months, after the judgment, dismissal, order, or proceeding was taken." Default was entered on June 23, 2021. Defendant did not file this motion to set aside until February 4, 2022, more than six months later. "The six-month time limitation is jurisdictional; the court has no power to grant relief under section 473 once the time has lapsed." (Austin v. Los Angeles Unified School Dist. (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 918, 918; see also Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 42 ["The six-month time limit for granting statutory relief is jurisdictional and the court may not consider a motion for relief made after that period has elapsed . . . The six-month period runs from entry of default."].)

Even if Defendant's motion could be deemed to have been filed on December 21, 2021, when he first attempted to file it on insufficient statutory notice, the unexplained delay of more than five months since learning of the entry of default is unreasonable as a matter of law. "A delay is unreasonable as a matter of law . . . when it exceeds three months and there is no evidence to explain the delay." (Minick v. City of Petaluma (2016) 3 Cal.App.5th 15, 34; see also Pulte Homes Corp. v. Williams Mechanical, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal.App.5th 267, 277-278 [trial court abused its discretion in granting motion to set aside default judgment where defendant delayed four and one-half months before seeking relief and therefore "did not show diligence"].)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/SRB)
